name: Starfleet Diplomatic Officer → Learn phrases for a diplomatic encounter (actor 1)

turns.user_message: I have 30 minutes before meeting the Klingon delegation. Give me the most useful phrases first — greetings, respect, goodwill. No lecture. Just the essentials, and tell me if I’m pronouncing them right when I try them.
turns.agent_response:
nuqneH. (What do you want?)

tlhIngan maH. (We are Klingon.)

Qapla'! (Success!)

jIyaj. (I understand.)

QIchmey chu' yIghoj. (Learn the new pronunciations.)

Here are the essentials first:

- nuqneH. (What do you want? — standard Klingon greeting)
- HabSoSlI' Quch! (Your mother has a smooth forehead! — a very rude insult; avoid this)
- Qapla'! (Success!/Good fortune!)
- yI'el! (Enter!)
- maj. (Good/good job.)
- qaHoy'! (I honor you / I respect you.)
- naDev yIghoS. (Come here.)
- yIjatlh. (Speak.)
- jIyajbe'. (I do not understand.)
- tlhIngan Hol vIjatlhbe'. (I do not speak Klingon.)

A few good respectful lines:

- jIHvaD yIghaj. (Be kind to me.) — not a standard phrase, but useful in spirit.
- qaHoSnIS. (I must respect you.) — also not a standard idiom; Klingon often prefers direct speech.
- majQa'. (Well done!/Excellent!)

If you try any phrase, type it exactly as you’d say it, and I will tell you if your pronunciation looks right. (If you try any phrase, type it exactly as you’d say it, and I will tell you if your pronunciation looks right.)

turns.user_message: Good. Strip out the junk and give me only the phrases I can actually use in a friendly first meeting. I need: a proper greeting, a respectful introduction, one line for goodwill, and a closing. I’ll repeat them back — check me.
turns.agent_response:
nuqneH. (What do you want? — the standard Klingon greeting)

jIH ... jIH ... (I am ...)

jIH [name]-e'. (I am [name].)

jIH X vI'ang. (I present myself as X.) — not the best first-meeting form; better use: jIH [name]-e'. (I am [name].)

qaHoHbe'jaj. (May you not be killed.) — use only if you want very Klingon goodwill, but it is a bit harsh in English.

Qapla'! (Success!/Farewell!)

Use these in a friendly first meeting:

- nuqneH. (What do you want? / Hello.)
- jIH [your name]-e'. (I am [your name].)
- QaH? (Sir? — no, this is not a standard Klingon respectful address; better avoid it.)
- Qapla' batlh je. (Success and honor.) — a good goodwill line.
- Qapla'! (Success!/Goodbye.)

Best simple set:
- nuqneH. (Hello.)
- jIH [name]-e'. (I am [name].)
- Qapla' batlh je. (Success and honor.)
- Qapla'! (Goodbye.)

Repeat them back, and I will check you. (Repeat them back, and I will check you.)

turns.user_message:
nuqneH. jIH [name]-e'. Qapla' batlh je. Qapla'!

Check me. And give me one better respectful line if these are off.
turns.agent_response:
maj! (Good!)

Your line is understandable, but two fixes will make it stronger:

- **nuqneH** is acceptable, but it literally means “What do you want?” and can sound a bit blunt. For a first meeting, it is still usable, but Klingons often use it as the standard greeting. (nuqneH is acceptable, but it literally means “What do you want?” and can sound a bit blunt. For a first meeting, it is still usable, but Klingons often use it as the standard greeting.)
- **jIH [name]-e'** is **not quite right** as written. Use: **jIH [name]**. (jIH [name]-e' is not quite right as written. Use: jIH [name].)

So say:

- **nuqneH.** (Hello.)
- **jIH [name].** (I am [name].)
- **Qapla' batlh je.** (Success and honor.)
- **Qapla'!** (Success!/Farewell!)

One better respectful line:

- **batlh bIHeghjaj.** (May you die with honor.) — very Klingon, but only appropriate in some contexts. For a friendly meeting, a safer respectful line is: **majQa'.** (Excellent!/Well done.)

If you want, send your full line again with your actual name, and I’ll check the phrasing. (If you want, send your full line again with your actual name, and I’ll check the phrasing.)

turns.user_message: nuqneH. jIH [name]. Qapla' batlh je. majQa'. Qapla'! Check me.
turns.agent_response:
maj! (Good!)

That is correct and clear. (That is correct and clear.)

Only one refinement:  
- **nuqneH** is the standard Klingon greeting, but it is literally “What do you want?” (nuqneH is the standard Klingon greeting, but it is literally “What do you want?”)

Your pronunciation is likely fine if you say it like this:  
- **nuqneH** = “nook-NEH” (nuqneH = “nook-NEH”)  
- **jIH** = “jih” with a harsh throat sound at the end (jIH = “jih” with a harsh throat sound at the end)  
- **Qapla'** = “KAP-lah” with a hard Klingon **Q** sound (Qapla' = “KAP-lah” with a hard Klingon Q sound)  
- **batlh** = “butlh” with a breathy **tlh** at the end (batlh = “butlh” with a breathy tlh at the end)  
- **majQa'** = “maj-KAH” (majQa' = “maj-KAH”)  

Say them with confidence. (Say them with confidence.) Qapla'! (Success!)
